frameworks.name: selective integration
frameworks.one_line: Successive attributes compete for processing; integration biased toward locally preferred attribute

frameworks.name: sequential sampling / evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Decisions follow integration of attribute information across alternatives

frameworks.name: unbiased integration
frameworks.one_line: Rival model without local-preference bias used for comparison

frameworks.name: optimality under late noise
frameworks.one_line: Bias is optimal when post-sensory noise corrupts decisions

core_question: Do posterior neural signals encode locally preferred attributes with higher gain during sequential value integration?
key_assumptions: Attributes are processed in turn rather than simultaneously
Attributes compete for limited processing resources
Posterior cortex contralateral signals index attribute encoding
manipulations: Two simultaneous streams of bars with varying heights
Judge higher vs lower average height across sessions
Compare selective-integration model vs unbiased rival model
Single-trial analysis of contralateral posterior EEG
key_findings: Selective integration model fit choice data better than unbiased rival
Contralateral posterior EEG covaried more steeply with decision information from locally preferred attributes
Neural gain asymmetry tracks the behavioral bias predicted by the model
interpretations: Posterior cortex implements gain modulation favoring locally preferred attributes
Selective integration is realized as differential neural gain rather than information loss alone
